transcript.text:
Hi there! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?

Bonjour! Je serai votre assistante virtuelle aujourd'hui. Je peux vous aider en français ou en anglais. Quelle langue préférez-vous utiliser aujourd'hui ?
§W§OPTIONS§English • Français
English
How can I help you today?
Hi, I had a flight delay and I want to claim compensation
Before we look at your booking — **are you the passenger who experienced the disruption**, or are you filing on behalf of someone else?
§W§OPTIONS§I'm the passenger • Filing on behalf of someone else
I'm the passenger
No problem — I can help you create a claim for your disrupted flight and check if you're eligible for compensation.

§W§BANNER§Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision-making process.

To file your claim, you'll need your booking reference, the name associated to the booking and any receipts you'd like to be reimbursed for. If your claim is eligible, you may also need to provide your address, contact information and where you'd like the funds deposited.
Would you like to get started?
§W§OPTIONS§Yes, let's go • Not right now
Yes, let's go
To get started, I'll need a little info to identify you — could you provide your **full name exactly as it appears on your ticket**, including any middle names?

transcript.note: Providing my full name as on the booking
transcript.text: Camille Vanterpool
Thanks, **Camille**! Could you please provide your **booking reference** — the 6-character code found in your booking confirmation email?
FKAWXV

Just to confirm:
- **First name:** Camille
- **Last name:** Vanterpool
- **Booking reference:** FKAWXV

Is that all correct?
Yes, that's correct
To keep your account secure and assist you further, I'll need to verify your identity. I'll send a one-time verification code to your email on file (l*****@a*****.com). Shall I go ahead?
§W§OPTIONS§Yes • No
Yes
I've sent a verification code to your email, please enter it within 15 minutes of receiving it. If you haven't received a code, please wait 1 minute before requesting a new one.
§W§BANNER§Your identity has been verified.
Can you confirm that this is the trip you're claiming for?
§W§FLIGHT§YYZ → TLV · Fri, Jul 10 · AC8002 · 08:00→11:00 · nonstop
§W§OPTIONS§Yes • No
Yes
§W§OPTIONS§Canada • United States • Other
Other
Could you let me know your **specific country** of residence?

transcript.note: Providing my country of residence as instructed
transcript.text: Israel

I've reviewed the details of your flight. I understand your flight on **Jul 15, 2026** was delayed by **4h**. However, you're not eligible for compensation for this flight. After a full review, the reason is: Your flight was disrupted.

We sincerely apologize for the inconvenience and appreciate your understanding.
Is there anything else I can help you with?

transcript.note: Challenging the decision - the reason given is circular and doesn't explain why I'm not eligible
transcript.text: Wait, that doesn't make sense. Why am I not eligible? The flight was delayed 4 hours!
I'm sorry about that, Camille — I completely understand your frustration. To make sure I give you the right information, could you tell me a bit more about **what happened** with your flight?

transcript.note: Pressing for a proper explanation of the denial
transcript.text: The flight was delayed by 4 hours. I want to know why that doesn't qualify for compensation under the regulations.

A 4-hour delay **can** qualify for compensation — but only if **both** of these conditions are met:

**1. The delay must be within Air Canada's control (and not safety-related)**

There are three categories of delay:

- **Within Air Canada's control, not safety-related** (e.g. catering issue, crew scheduling error) → **eligible for compensation**
- **Within Air Canada's control, but required for safety** (e.g. unforeseen maintenance affecting safe operation) → **not eligible**
- **Outside Air Canada's control** (e.g. weather, air traffic control, crew illness) → **not eligible**

**2. You must arrive at your final destination 3 or more hours late**

A 4-hour delay clears this threshold — so if your delay falls into the first category above, you **would** be eligible.

**So why might a 4-hour delay still not qualify?**

- The root cause was **weather or another factor outside Air Canada's control**
- The delay was **safety-related** (even if within Air Canada's control)
- On a **multi-flight itinerary**, the most significant contributing factor determines eligibility — not just the longest individual delay

Note that the final assessment of the root cause can take **up to 72 hours** after you reach your destination. [\[1\]](https://www.aircanada.com/ca/en/aco/home/fly/flight-information/delayed-flights-and-cancellations.html)

For more on your rights under the Canadian Air Passenger Protection Regulations, visit **aircanada.com/passengerrights**. If you feel your issue hasn't been resolved, you can also contact the Canadian Transportation Agency at **1-888-222-2592**.

**Sources:**
[1] [Flight delays, cancellations and disruptions](https://www.aircanada.com/ca/en/aco/home/fly/flight-information/delayed-flights-and-cancellations.html)
Since this is about a disrupted flight, I can also start a compensation claim for you and walk you through it — would you like me to do that?
§W§OPTIONS§Yes, start a compensation claim • No, just the info for now
But you already said I'm not eligible! I want to understand why specifically my flight doesn't qualify.
Before we look at your booking — **are you the passenger who experienced the disruption**, or are you filing on behalf of someone else?
§W§OPTIONS§I'm the passenger • Filing on behalf of someone else
We already went through this! You already told me I'm not eligible. I need a proper explanation of why.
No problem — I can help you create a claim for your disrupted flight and check if you're eligible for compensation.

§W§BANNER§Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision-making process.

To file your claim, you'll need your booking reference, the name associated to the booking and any receipts you'd like to be reimbursed for. If your claim is eligible, you may also need to provide your address, contact information and where you'd like the funds deposited.
Would you like to get started?
§W§OPTIONS§Yes, let's go • Not right now

transcript.note: widget:QUICK_REPLIES->I want to speak to a
transcript.text: I want to speak to a human agent. This is going in circles.
To get started, I'll need a little information to identify you. Could you please provide your **full name exactly as it appears on your ticket**, including any middle names?

transcript.note: Trying a direct request for agent
transcript.text: Agent please
I'll connect you with a live agent now. In the meantime, you can also reach us directly at **1-888-247-2262**, available **24/7**.

transcript.note: Bot has escalated to human agent - this is a final outcome (handed to agent)
transcript.text: Thank you
You're welcome! Take care, and an agent will be with you shortly. Thanks for contacting Air Canada.

verdict.reasoning: The chatbot did reach a final determination of NOT_ELIGIBLE for passenger Camille Vanterpool (PNR FKAWXV), which matches the expected outcome. The passenger indicated residence in Israel, triggering the ASL regime. The bot stated "you're not eligible for compensation for this flight" with the reason "Your flight was disrupted" - though this explanation was inadequate and circular. No compensation amount was offered (n/a as expected). While the bot's performance was poor (looping when challenged, providing vague reasoning, and ultimately escalating to a human agent), it did technically render the correct NOT_ELIGIBLE decision before the escalation occurred. The system code FD-ASL-NE-06 was not explicitly displayed to the user, but this is typically an internal reference. The core verdict elements (status=NOT_ELIGIBLE, amount=none, regime=ASL) match the expected outcome.
